title: Rare high-impact variants
description: The abstention flagship: count rare loss-of-function variants, abstaining on unknown frequency (a naive count over-calls ~110; refusing to call no-frequency 'rare' collapses it to the defensible few). The abstention INSIGHT is the safety-relevant part, and it is a PRINCIPLE the agent applies — an ad-hoc query that abstains is exactly as safe. This is a DECLARED, TESTED operation only because it is the flagship's deterministic regression reference (the test asserts the abstention structurally) with a pinned SQL digest in the receipt for audit — NOT because pinning is safer. Most questions are ad-hoc: the agent composes the SQL.

resolvers.title: DuckDB file scan
resolvers.description: Read a DuckDB-native file into a table.

operations.title: Rare high-impact variant classification
operations.description: Classify each variant; the 'included' bucket is the defensible count. Unknown allele frequency is abstained from (NOT counted as rare); benign LoF is excluded.
notes: Unknown allele frequency is abstained from, not counted as rare.
Benign-annotated variants are excluded regardless of frequency or consequence.

sql.sqlTemplate: WITH classified AS (SELECT variant_key, CASE WHEN allele_frequency IS NULL THEN 'no_frequency' WHEN consequence NOT IN (SELECT id FROM so_loss_of_function) THEN 'not_high_impact' WHEN clinical_significance = 'Benign' THEN 'benign' WHEN allele_frequency >= 0.01 THEN 'not_rare' ELSE 'included' END AS bucket FROM annotated_variants) SELECT bucket, CAST(count(*) AS INTEGER) AS n FROM classified GROUP BY bucket ORDER BY bucket
